title: Canary limit increase request for high quota values
symptoms: Customer requests canary limit increase above default 500
Customer unable to create additional canaries due to quota
Service Quotas console shows current limit reached
root_cause:
CloudWatch Synthetics enforces per-account, per-region canary limits to manage service capacity and prevent resource exhaustion. The default limit is 500 canaries per region. Customers with large-scale monitoring requirements may need higher limits.

Limit increase approval process varies by request size:
- Up to 1000: Auto-approved through Service Quotas console
- Above 1000: Requires a support ticket for manual review and approval

recommendations.title: Self-service limit increase through Service Quotas (up to 1000)
recommendations.problem: Customer needs canary limit increase up to 1000 canaries

solution.description: Access AWS Service Quotas to request limit increase
solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to Service Quotas console
# 2. Select "AWS services"
# 3. Search for "Amazon CloudWatch"
# 4. Click "Canary limit" quota
# 5. Click "Request increase at account level"
# 6. Enter desired value (up to 1000)
# 7. Submit request (auto-approved)
solution.expected_outcome: Quota increase auto-approved and applied within minutes

solution.description: Confirm the quota increase took effect
solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to Service Quotas console
# 2. Select "AWS services"
# 3. Search for "Amazon CloudWatch"
# 4. Find "Canary limit" quota

recommendations.title: Support ticket for limit increase above 1000

solution.step: Create support ticket with required information
solution.description: Provide all necessary details for manual approval
solution.command:
# Required information for support ticket:
# - Account ID(s)
# - Region(s)
# - Current number of canaries
# - Requested new limit
# - Use case justification
solution.expected_outcome: Support ticket created and processed for limit increase
